origin.name: Latvia
origin.key_document: Mirsanas aplieciba (death certificate, requires certified English translation)
origin.source: FCDO Latvia travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Riga guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Latvia is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is often simpler than full body repatriation. You will need the Mirsanas aplieciba, cremation certificate, and urn transport documentation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Mirsanas aplieciba (death certificate, certified English translation required)
special_rules: Latvia is an EU member and Hague Apostille Convention member, which simplifies document legalisation.
Death must be registered with the Dzimtsarakstu departaments (Civil Registry Department) promptly.
All Latvian-language documentation requires a certified English translation for UK acceptance.
The Prosecutor's Office orders post-mortem for suspicious or violent deaths.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Latvian documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Riga can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Latvia to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Latvia. Repatriation to the UK takes 1-2 weeks. Civil Registry, apostille, and Latvian translation all handled. Contact us 24/7.
